title: Rain Sensor — Resistive / Capacitive Detection Module
content: A resistive rain sensor uses interdigitated PCB traces: water bridges traces reducing resistance from >1 MΩ (dry) to <10 kΩ (wet). An LM393 comparator converts the resistance change to a digital HIGH/LOW output for microcontroller input.
extended_content: Types: 1) Resistive (PCB comb trace) — simple, low cost < £1, 3.3–5 V, digital + analog outputs. 2) Optical (IR refraction) — detects droplets on glass via total internal reflection change, used in automotive wipers. 3) Capacitive — fringe capacitance increases with water film. Response time: resistive 50–200 ms. Sensitivity set by LM393 potentiometer threshold. Operating range: 3.3–5 V, current 15–20 mA. False triggers: condensation, dust. PCB maintenance: clean with isopropanol. Arduino: analogRead > threshold triggers servo umbrella deployment or wipers.
formula: R_wet < 10 kΩ (V_out = LOW) ;  R_dry > 1 MΩ (V_out = HIGH)
source: Arduino Rain Sensor Module Datasheet; Bosch RBSS Rain/Light/Solar Sensor Technical Documentation; IEEE Sensors Journal
